claims.text: The variant NM_000152.5:c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) is annotated with ClinVar ID VCV000004029 in the expanded variant nomenclature footnote of Table 2.

The variant c.1935C>A appears in cis with c.1726G>A as a haplotype [c.1935C>A;1726G>A]; in Cohort 1 of the Mini-COMET trial (IOPD patients), this haplotype is found in two patients, each in trans with a different second allele (c.784G>A and c.1062C>G;1286A>G, respectively).

In Cohort 2 of the Mini-COMET trial (IOPD patients), the haplotype [c.1935C>A;1726G>A] appears in two patients: one compound heterozygous with c.2842INST on the opposite allele, and one with the same [c.1935C>A;1726G>A] haplotype on both alleles.
The co-occurring variant c.1726G>A in the same haplotype is annotated as p.Gly576Ser with ClinVar ID VCV000092467.

Patients carrying the c.1935C>A-containing haplotype were IOPD patients enrolled in the Mini-COMET trial, aged 1–12 years, who had previously received alglucosidase alfa for ≥6 months and demonstrated clinical decline or sub-optimal response; all patients across both cohorts had cardiomegaly at baseline.

citations.quote: a Phase 2 trial (Mini-COMET) enrolled patients (n = 22) aged 1–12 years who had previously received a stable dose of alglucosidase alfa for ≥6 months and demonstrated clinical decline or sub-optimal response
